text: Tunisia have adopted a more 'progressive' brand of football relative to their defensively focused football they've played at previous tournaments, and surprise surprise this is the almost inevitable result. International football is about managing your resources, you can't go out and get better players beyond convincing dual nationals so it's better to play a style of football that's better suited to what you actually have. It's no coincidence that they conceded five against Belgium and three against Nigeria who aren't even qualified.

text: After watching the whole game, I came away thinking Brazil's attacking talent isn't the problem. Their forwards can still create moments out of nothing, but the build-up from midfield into the final third wasn't clean enough. I still think Brazil are contenders, but there are definitely some warning signs beneath the result.
